title: NASA's Plan to Use a Space Station Part for a Nuclear Mission
summary: NASA has a new plan to use a part from a space station called Gateway for a nuclear-powered mission to Mars. Gateway was being built to orbit the Moon, but NASA is changing its focus to build a base on the Moon instead. The part, called the Power and Propulsion Element, is almost ready and will be used for a mission called SR-1 Freedom. This mission will test a nuclear reactor in space to help spacecraft travel farther, like to Mars. Nuclear power is useful in space because it works even where sunlight is weak. NASA hopes to launch SR-1 Freedom by 2028 to show that nuclear propulsion can work. This is important because the U.S. has only flown one nuclear reactor in space over 60 years ago, and new technology could make space travel faster and more efficient.

background_read: NASA is changing its plans for space exploration. Instead of building a space station around the Moon called Gateway, they want to focus on making a base on the Moon's surface. This means they need to find a new use for parts of Gateway that are already built. One big part is the Power and Propulsion Element, which can help spacecraft move. NASA plans to use it for a mission called SR-1 Freedom to test nuclear power in space. Nuclear power can help spacecraft travel to far places like Mars because it doesn't need sunlight. This is exciting because it could make space travel better in the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: NASA, the U.S. space agency
WHAT: Using a part from Gateway for a nuclear-powered mission called SR-1 Freedom
WHERE: In space, aiming for missions to Mars
WHY: To test nuclear propulsion for faster and more efficient space travel

perspectives.description: NASA sees this as a smart way to reuse Gateway parts and advance space technology with nuclear power.
